questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY), how does the Central Sector Scheme (CS) component differ from the Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) component?

options.text: CS is implemented by the States with 100% central funding, while CSS is implemented by the Centre.
CS is fully funded and implemented by the Central Government, while CSS is partially supported by the Centre and implemented by the States.
CS focuses only on marine fisheries, while CSS focuses only on inland aquaculture.
CS is restricted to Union Territories, while CSS is restricted to the States.

questions.explanation: According to the scheme structure, the Central Sector Scheme (CS) is fully funded and implemented by the Central Government. The Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS) is partially supported by the Central Government and implemented by the states.
questions.takeaway: PMMSY utilizes both CS and CSS models to balance central oversight with state-level implementation.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Central Sector Scheme (CS): Fully funded and implemented by Central Government. Centrally Sponsored Scheme (CSS): Partially supported by the Central Government and implemented by the states.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: One of the key economic objectives of the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) is the reduction of post-harvest losses. In this regard, what is the specific target and current achievement status mentioned in the scheme?
questions.explanation: The target set under PMMSY for post-harvest losses is to reduce them from the reported 20-25% to about 10%. As of December 2024, the achievement is a reduction to 10-15%.
questions.takeaway: PMMSY aims to significantly cut post-harvest losses by half (from ~20-25% to 10%).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Targets: Reduction of post-harvest losses from the reported 20-25% to about 10%. Achievements: Post-harvest losses reduced to 10-15% (As of Dec 2024).

questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: The recently launched Pradhan Mantri Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDDKY) is primarily aimed at:

options.text: Providing insurance coverage for post-harvest losses specifically in the fisheries sector.
Revolutionizing agriculture by making it more productive, sustainable, and financially rewarding.
Creating a digital registry for all small and marginal farmers to facilitate direct benefit transfers.
Promoting the export of traditional Indian grains to global markets through specialized trade hubs.

questions.explanation: The PM Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDDKY) was launched to revolutionize Indian agriculture by focusing on productivity, sustainability, and financial rewards for farmers.
questions.takeaway: PMDDKY is a new initiative focused on the broader agricultural sector's productivity and sustainability.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The Prime Minister launched The Pradhan Mantri Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDDKY) initiative to revolutionize Indian agriculture by making it more productive, sustainable, and financially rewarding for farmers.
